_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to design and justify a physical activity plan that includes strategies for being more active and spending less time sitting.

_source.w: We are learning to design and justify a physical activity plan that includes strategies for being more active and spending less time sitting.
y: Personalised planning puts us in control of our own health and fitness.
Justifying our plan develops evidence-based reasoning skills.
These planning skills transfer to many areas of life — study, career, personal goals.
eg: Designing a weekly physical activity plan tailored to our interests, abilities, and schedule.
Justifying our choice of activities using knowledge of fitness components and health guidelines.
Including strategies for reducing sedentary time — active transport, standing breaks, screen time limits.
Setting SMART goals for our physical activity plan.
Implementing the plan for two weeks and evaluating its effectiveness.
Adjusting our plan based on what we learned during implementation.
